task_completion.description: Open and read the specification document containing the formulas and edge cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) is open displaying the contents of 'chiller_cop_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Create the required Real/Float tags in Crimson 3.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data Tags section in Crimson 3.0 shows the tags: Flow_L_s, Return_T, Supply_T, Comp1_kW, Comp2_kW, and Sys_COP.

task_completion.description: Create a program named 'Calculate_COP' with the logic to calculate the COP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Programs section in Crimson 3.0 shows a program named 'Calculate_COP' and the code editor contains logic referencing the created tags.

task_completion.description: Export the program code to Calculate_COP.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window shows 'Calculate_COP.txt' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\CrimsonTasks', or a text editor shows the exported code.

task_completion.description: Save the Crimson 3.0 project file as ammonia_chiller_final.c3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Crimson 3.0 window title bar displays 'ammonia_chiller_final.c3' or a file explorer shows the file in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Crimson 3.0 GUI to create tags and programs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Crimson 3.0 interface (Data Tags and Programs panes) rather than just writing text files via terminal.

integrity.description: The exported program code matches the logic created in Crimson 3.0.
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of 'Calculate_COP.txt' appear to be genuine Crimson 3.0 program code that matches what was visible in the Crimson 3.0 program editor.

integrity.description: The project was saved properly through the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used the File -> Save or Save As dialog in Crimson 3.0 to save the .c3 file, rather than creating an empty file via terminal commands.
